channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Billionaire Mark Cuban says it’s ‘embarrassing’ to not pay employees well—and a $20 minimum wage should be standard
item.description: Billionaire entrepreneur Mark Cuban says he’s helped mint ‘at least a thousand millionaires’, and that more employers should share success with staff.

item.title: Trump rings opening bell for Trump Accounts as Treasury commits $1.4 billion in seed money
item.description: For the first time in history, a president rang the opening bell at the Oval Office, and Trump did so to champion the launch of Trump Accounts.

item.title: The yen is quietly crashing as Japan’s debt crisis bleeds into currency markets, and efforts to halt the slide are ‘doomed to fail,’ economist says
item.description: "There’ll come a point when markets will just ignore intervention."

item.title: SpaceX’s supervoting shares revive a decades-old governance debate
item.description: Buffett and Alphabet’s founders used similar supervoting power to fund long-term bets, while many examples show entrenchment of failed leadership.
